name: Kentucky First Federal Bancorp
summary: Kentucky First Federal Bancorp is a mid-tier savings institution holding company operating two community-oriented savings banks in Kentucky: First Federal Savings and Loan Association of Hazard and First Federal Savings Bank of Kentucky. The company primarily attracts deposits from the general public and originates adjustable-rate and fixed-rate mortgage loans secured by one- to four-family residential real estate, as well as home equity loans, church loans, commercial real estate, and multi-family property loans.

products.name: Adjustable-Rate Residential Mortgage Loans
products.description: Loans indexed to MIRS Transition Index or 1-Year CMT, adjusted annually, with lifetime caps.

products.name: Fixed-Rate Mortgage Loans
products.description: Fixed-rate mortgages with terms up to 30 years, originated primarily for sale on the secondary market.

products.name: Home Equity Loans
products.description: Loans secured by home equity offered to consumers in the Banks' market areas.

products.name: Deposit Accounts
products.description: Savings and deposit products offered to the general public, insured by the FDIC.

products.name: Church and Commercial Real Estate Loans
products.description: Loans secured by churches, multi-family properties, and professional office buildings.

partnerships.partner_name: Federal Home Loan Bank of Cincinnati
partnerships.description: Both Banks are members, providing access to one of 11 regional FHLB system banks for funding.
